name: Haggis (Scotch-canadian)
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 lb Pork fat or salt pork
recipeInstructions:
This New Brunswick recipe reflects a change in the traditional haggis - a change which recent Scottish arrivals consider akin to sacrilege. Directions:
1. Grease a 9x5x3 inch loaf pan.
2. Cut pork fat into cubes and fry off the fat from the pork or salt pork. Pour off the grease as it accumulates. When the pieces are golden brown and crisp, they are called "cracklings" in Ontario, or "kips" in the Maritimes.
3. Drain well and cool.
4. Wash the pork liver and place in a large pot. Cover with boiling water and boil for about 1 hour, until a fork can easily be inserted. Remove liver and allow to cool. Reserve liquid.
5. Put cooled liver and 2 cups of cracklings through the food grinder. Mix together.
6. Stir in the oats, salt, and pepper. Add sufficient cooking liquid to hold the mixture together.
7. Press into prepared loaf pan, cover with waxed paper and foil.
8. Steam for 1 hour. Cool.
9. To Serve: Slice 1/2 inch thick and pan fry until golden brown on both sides. Serve piping hot.
